mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad demand contraction, forcing equity repricing over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% month-over-month during inflationary periods signals capacity constraints that trigger monetary tightening fears, depressing short-term equity prices.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation supply expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising inflation and stagnant federal funds rates creates real rate compression, prompting equity market volatility and negative forward returns as investors price delayed policy response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months exhibiting this inflation-policy divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers recession risk premiums, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters following a GDP contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during a low unemployment regime signals supply constraints that prevent monetary easing, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exceed zero in >60% of months when industrial production falls below trend during tight labor markets.
